key_insight: MXtalGFlow turns molecular crystal generation from isolated structure proposals into sampling a thermodynamically meaningful Boltzmann distribution over crystal packings.
engineering_relevance: This gives crystal structure prediction a principled way to rank, compare, and discover polymorphs and competing packing motifs for molecular materials design.
method: Defines a canonical crystal parameterization for molecular crystals and space groups.
Trains energy-based GFlowNets to match a target Boltzmann distribution over crystal structures.
Analyzes sampled distributions around known polymorphs and explores alternative low-energy basins.
evidence_signal: The framework is evaluated on two molecules under two energy models (Lennard-Jones and the Universal Model for Atoms), showing distributions around known polymorphs and additional competitive packing modes.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen energy function and canonical parameterization, so physical fidelity and transferability must be checked carefully for new molecules or force fields.
paper_title: A Distributional Framework for Generative Modeling of Molecular Crystals
